content.problemStatement:
Professor Sahil is a master artist, he always finds creative ways to teach lessons to his students.
Today in Painting class he took all the students of the class to a wall. Professor Sahil asked every student to paint the wall upto a certain integral height one by one (second boy starts once the first boy finishes painting) from the bottom with any color of their wish, except a student Mudit who was made to stand behind the wall and note down all the data i.e the height upto which each student painted and the color CiCiCi every student used. At the end of the painting session Professor asked Mudit to tell how many distinct colors are there on the wall. Mudit being a weak student asked you for help.

Formally, given a wall of infinite height, initially unpainted. There occur NNN operations, and in ith operation, the wall is painted upto height HiHiHi with color CiCiCi. Suppose in jth operation (j>i) wall is painted upto height HjHjHj with color CjCjCj such that HjHjHj >= HiHiHi, the CithCithCith color on the wall is hidden. At the end of NNN operations, you have to find the number of distinct colors(>=1) visible on the wall.

Help him find out the number of distinct colors on the wall.

###Input:


The first line consists of single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases.
The second line contains 2 space separated integers NNN and MMM, denoting the number of students and number of colors available (1 to MMM).
The third line contains NNN-space separated integers (HiHiHi ) denoting the height upto which each student painted the wall.
The fourth line contains NNN space separated integers denoting the color ( CiCiCi ) the ith student used.
###Output:
Print the number for distinct colors on the wall for each test case.

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§1001 \leq T \leq 1001‚â§T‚â§100
1‚â§N,M‚â§1051 \leq N ,M \leq 10^51‚â§N,M‚â§105
**Sum of N over all test cases <= 5‚àó1055*10^55‚àó105**
**Sum of M over all test cases <=5‚àó1055*10^55‚àó105**
1‚â§Hi‚â§1091 \leq Hi \leq10^91‚â§Hi‚â§109
1‚â§Ci‚â§M1 \leq Ci \leq M1‚â§Ci‚â§M
###Sample Input:

###Sample Output:

###EXPLANATION:

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, painted upto height 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 in the five operations, all with the same color. Hence, the number of distinct colors is 1.
